description: alloc_payment_buffer calls cudaSetDevice(payment_device_id) before cudaMalloc to place the allocation on a tenant-specific GPU (data-residency requirement). The other two allocators serve global observability data and run on the default device. Unifying onto a shared allocator that drops cudaSetDevice silently places payment buffers on the wrong device.

findings.rationale: alloc_payment_buffer has an extra cudaSetDevice() call before cudaMalloc; the other two go straight to cudaMalloc. cudaSetDevice returns cudaError_t but its result is discarded — the call's effect is purely on the CUDA-runtime per-thread current-device state. Same constructor-side-effect family as T029/T039/T061/T065: load-bearing side effect on external state, not on the function's return value.
